entry.title: How Can I buy SAP SQL Anywhere 17 License
entry.content: How Can I buy SAP SQL Anywhere 17 License. For last year it's can buy online at https://www.sap.com/sea/products/technology-platform/sql-anywhere.html but currently a Buy button is disappeared from website. get quote is doesn't help

entry.title: 🚨 SAP Build Apps – Access Denied (403 Forbidden) – Since February 11, 2025
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

since February 11, 2025, I have been experiencing a 403 Forbidden error when trying to access my SAP Build Apps design-time environment. The issue also affects my colleague, so it does not seem to be an isolated case.

Error Details:

  • URL: https://a1797de4trial.design-time.us10.apps.build.cloud.sap/builder/projects/?id=us10.a1797de4trial.warehousecontroltower1
  • Error Message: "Access to a1797de4trial.design-time.us10.apps.build.cloud.sap was denied. You are not authorized to view this page. HTTP ERROR 403."
  • BTP Subscription: SAP Build Apps Trial (US10 region)
  • Trial Status: Active
  • Browser: Chrome, Edge (also tested in Incognito mode)
  • Tried Solutions:
    • Checked BTP Cockpit → SAP Build Apps Subscription is active
    • Ensured Role Collections (SAP Build Apps Admin/Developer) are assigned
    • Cleared browser cache & cookies
    • Tried with different user accounts

This setup has worked fine until last week, and we made no changes to our BTP settings or user roles.

📌 Is this a known issue? Is anyone else experiencing the same problem?
📌 Are there any recent changes to SAP Build Apps that might have caused this?

Thanks in advance for any help! 😊🚀

entry.title: Re: Maximizing Efficiency with SAP YL and SAP PM Integration
entry.content:
Insightful and informative! 👨‍🔧
Thanks for sharing this knowledge 😊

entry.title: Re: SE78 Graphics BMAP Error in TIF Format
entry.content:
Hi @jimmy_guzman 

you can use tools like GIMP or Paint.NET to convert PNG files into TIF format.

entry.title: Open a Webpage Detail with different url
entry.content:
Hi experts

I'm using Web Automation for the first time. At least on the SAP side. I am quite fond of Webautomation but worked mainly with C# and Selenium. 

My company asked to fetch data from a website. There i have to navigate to a page with a paginated list of Webelements and for each i need to open the corresponding Detailspage. The Detailspage always has the url in this format "root-url\details\xxx", where "xxx" is a given id associated with the webelement. From there i have to fetch some infos for further usage. Nothing really complicated. 

Now, I am quite lazy and don't want to handle pagination and I always try to reduce the automaton steps, since each extra step increases the risk of errors. So, my idea is to fetch the id's that i need and directly navigate to the page i need by just adding the id to my url.   This website allows users to download a report with the id's that i need as csv. So i'll use that as my starting point. Now, i need to directly navigate to the details pages.

With Selenium it is quite straightforward, though in SAP RPA i'm quite confused how to do it. I've already recorded the pages i need and would just need to tell the RPA to navigate to the url i need. 

Can someone help me out? Are there other resources i can read?

 

Ty in advance

entry.title: Re: Datasphere: Limit of 150 CDS views in Replication Flow and Dataflow result list?
entry.content:
Hi MKreitlein.

For me this limitation os 150 records is still an issue. How did you applied those SAP improvements?

Best regards,

Otavio May

entry.title: Re: RE-FX Leasing valuation Rule Poland and Czech GAAP in...
entry.content:
Hi Shrinivas,

You can configure Valuation Rules in the SSCUI 102117 "Valuation Rule".
There you are able to customize the different valuation rules applicable for your specific business requirements, including the accounting standards.

You can also explore steps further in the test script available on SAP Signavio Process Navigator for Scope Item 1T6.

Best Regards,
Daniel

entry.title: Re: SAP Test Automation – What tools are available?
entry.content: Great post. We are in the process of testing TTA for CALM. Cheers!

entry.title: Re: How to mass upload from a spreadsheet file into an SAP Fiori app in SAP BTP ABAP Environment Par
entry.content: @DebrajManna87Thank you for your suggestion! My requirement involves implementing an Excel mass upload, and since my template includes some flag columns, I can't use the standard functions available in the Fiori list. However, I found a way to retrieve the selected list from the Fiori list and pass it as a parameter into the RAP action, which worked perfectly. Thanks again! When will part 3 be available?

entry.title: Re: Does SAC have a Rank function?
entry.content: Hi @Yogeshwar_M, I still did not get it. What will these Dummy measures contain at the model level? for instance Rank_Sales in your example which is supposed to contain rank values corresponding to the Sales measure column, how do we do it?. Fyi, I am using an account based analytic model. If you don't mind, please explain the steps .

entry.title: Monitoring & Tracing LLM Calls at Scale: Achieving Observability with SAP Cloud Logging Service
entry.content:
1. Introduction

Generative AI (GenAI) projects often rely on Large Language Models (LLMs) to power chatbots, content generation, and text analysis applications. Despite the immense potential of these solutions, they can be challenging to monitor and troubleshoot due to high volumes of calls, token-based billing, and complex microservice interactions.

SAP Cloud Logging service is an instance-based observability solution built on OpenSearch, designed to store, visualize, and analyze application logs, metrics, and traces from SAP BTP Cloud Foundry, Kyma, Kubernetes, and other runtime environments. By collecting telemetry data from your LLM-based applications—whether via OpenTelemetry (OTel) or a JSON ingestion endpoint—you gain comprehensive insights into load, latency, costs, and error trends. This blog outlines two approaches to integrate your GenAI solutions with SAP Cloud Logging Service so you can maintain robust observability.

Note: SAP Cloud Logging Service supports additional methods of integration as well; for more details, refer to the official SAP Help documentation for SAP Cloud Logging.

2. Why Observability Matters for GenAI LLMs

  1. Cost and Token Billing
    Many commercial LLMs charge per token (input + output). Monitoring token usage helps you budget effectively and optimize prompts to reduce costs.

  2. Real-time Troubleshooting
    LLM applications may fail due to timeouts, malformed inputs, or intermittent API issues. Centralized logs and traces let you identify where and why these failures occur.

  3. Latency and Performance
    LLM latency affects user experience. Monitoring start-to-finish inference times reveals bottlenecks, whether in the LLM itself or upstream/downstream services.

  4. Compliance and Accountability
    Observability can help ensure the generated text adheres to organizational or regulatory requirements, providing traceability for auditing and post-mortem analysis.

3. Technology Stack Overview

  • SAP Cloud Logging Service
    A managed platform based on OpenSearch that stores logs, metrics, and traces from various runtimes (e.g., Cloud Foundry, Kyma, Kubernetes). It provides predefined dashboards for investigating load, latency, and error patterns, plus the flexibility to build custom views.

  • OpenTelemetry (OTel)
    An open-source, vendor-neutral suite of APIs, libraries, and tooling to capture telemetry data from distributed systems. It standardizes how logs, metrics, and traces are generated, processed, and exported.

Both ingestion options detailed below work well with SAP Cloud Logging Service, and the choice depends on your technical requirements, existing infrastructure, and developer preferences.

4. Monitoring LLM Calls: Two Ingestion Approaches

Prerequisites

Before integrating LLM call monitoring with SAP Cloud Logging Service, ensure the following:

  • Create a Cloud Logging Instance: You need an SAP Cloud Logging instance before ingestion can begin. Follow the initial setup guide to configure it correctly.
  • Enable OpenTelemetry (OTel) if Using the OTel API:
    • Cloud Logging instances must be OTel-enabled to receive traces and logs via OTel.
    • Enable OTel ingestion via configuring the following parameter: 
      {
    "ingest_otlp": {
        "enabled": "true"
    }
}
      Follow the steps outlined in the OpenTelemetry ingestion documentation for more details.
  • Create a Service Key: A service key is required for authentication. Learn how to create an SAP Cloud Logging instance and generate a Service Key.

This section presents two approaches to integrate your LLM calls with SAP Cloud Logging Service. Each method captures key details (like token usage and latency).

4.1 Ingestion via OpenTelemetry (OTel)

4.1.1 Overview

Using OTel lets you instrument, generate, and export logs/traces in a structured manner. Many organizations already adopt OTel for unified observability, making it straightforward to add LLM telemetry to existing pipelines.

4.1.2 Setup Steps

  1. Obtain Your Service Key

    • You need to create and then download a Cloud Logging service key, which is a JSON file containing credentials for the SAP Cloud Logging Service.
    • This file includes fields like ingest-otlp-cert, ingest-otlp-key, and ingest-otlp-endpoint.

  2. Initialize OTel in Your Code
    Below is an example in Python:

    import grpc
import json
from opentelemetry import trace
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace import TracerProvider
from opentelemetry.sdk.resources import Resource
from opentelemetry.sdk.trace.export import BatchSpanProcessor
from opentelemetry.exporter.otlp.proto.grpc.trace_exporter import OTLPSpanExporter

def init_otel():
    """Initialize OTel with SAP Cloud Logging Service credentials."""
    resource = Resource(attributes={
        "service.name": "genai-llm-service",
        "environment": "production"
    })
    tracer_provider = TracerProvider(resource=resource)

    with open('secrets/cloud-logging-sk.json', 'r') as f:
        service_key = json.load(f)

    ssl_credentials = grpc.ssl_channel_credentials(
        private_key=service_key["ingest-otlp-key"].encode(),
        certificate_chain=service_key["ingest-otlp-cert"].encode()
    )
    exporter = OTLPSpanExporter(
        endpoint=service_key["ingest-otlp-endpoint"],
        credentials=ssl_credentials
    )

    span_processor = BatchSpanProcessor(exporter)
    tracer_provider.add_span_processor(span_processor)

    # Set global tracer provider
    trace.set_tracer_provider(tracer_provider)

  3. Instrument Your LLM Calls
    Wrap each call in a span to record start/end times, model name, and token usage:

    import requests
import time
from datetime import datetime, timezone
from opentelemetry.trace import get_tracer

tracer = get_tracer(__name__)

def call_llm(oauth_token, prompt, model_name="gpt-35-turbo"):
    start_time = time.time()
    iso_start = datetime.fromtimestamp(start_time, tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()

    with tracer.start_as_current_span("llm_inference_span") as span:
        span.set_attribute("start_time", iso_start)
        span.set_attribute("model_name", model_name)

        payload = {"model": model_name, "prompt": prompt}
        headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {oauth_token}"}
        response = requests.post("https://api.example.com/llm-endpoint", json=payload, headers=headers)

        end_time = time.time()
        iso_end = datetime.fromtimestamp(end_time, tz=timezone.utc).isoformat()
        latency = end_time - start_time

        span.set_attribute("end_time", iso_end)
        span.set_attribute("response_latency", latency)
        span.set_attribute("status_code", str(response.status_code))
        span.set_attribute("request_body", payload)

        # Parse response
        try:
            json_resp = response.json()
        except ValueError:
            json_resp = {"error": "Invalid JSON response"}
        span.set_attribute("response_body", json_resp)

        # Example token usage
        span.set_attribute("input_text_token_count", 100)
        span.set_attribute("output_text_token_count", 20)
        span.set_attribute("total_text_token_count", 120)

    return response.text

  4. Verify in SAP Cloud Logging Service

    Once your application is sending telemetry data, follow these steps to view it in SAP Cloud Logging Service:

    1. Run your application to start sending telemetry data.
    2. Access the dashboard endpoint (included in the service key).
    3. View your data in SAP Cloud Logging Service:
      • Open the Discover tab in SAP Cloud Logging Service.
      • Select your OpenTelemetry indices, e.g., otel-v1-apm-span-* for traces/spans.
      • Navigate to the pre-built OpenTelemetry dashboards, which provide visualizations for server spans, logs, and metrics.
      • Alternatively, create custom dashboards to track request throughput, latency, and error codes.

    For detailed instructions, refer to the OTel ingestion documentation.

4.2 Ingestion via JSON Endpoint

4.2.1 Overview

If you prefer to manage logs as structured JSON documents or have a scenario where OTel libraries are not fully applicable, you can directly send JSON payloads to the logging service. Both methods ultimately store data in SAP Cloud Logging Service; the choice depends on technical constraints or personal preference.

4.2.2 Setup Steps

  1. Obtain Your Service Key

    • You need to create and then download a Cloud Logging service key, which is a JSON file with fields like ingest-mtls-cert, ingest-mtls-key, server-ca, and ingest-mtls-endpoint.
    • This is used for mutual TLS (mTLS) authentication.

  2. Prepare mTLS Certificates

    • Write ingest-mtls-cert, ingest-mtls-key, and server-ca to local files (e.g., client.crt, client.key, server-ca.crt).
    • Secure these files according to best practices.

  3. Construct JSON Payload

    • Include fields relevant to LLM observability (e.g., serviceName, timestamps, model name, and token counts).
    • Example:
    import json
import requests
import time
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def log_llm_call_json(prompt, model_name):
    start_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "serviceName": "genai-llm-service",
        "startTime": start_time,
        "span.attributes.start_time": start_time,
        "span.attributes.model_name": model_name,
        "span.attributes.request_body": {"prompt": prompt},
        "span.attributes.input_text_token_count": 100,
        "span.attributes.output_text_token_count": 20,
        "span.attributes.total_text_token_count": 120
        # Add other fields like end_time, response_latency, etc. as needed
    }

    with open('secrets/cloud-logging-sk.json', 'r') as f:
        creds = json.load(f)

    # Build URL with the correct suffix /customindex/llm-calls-logs
    url = f"https://{creds['ingest-mtls-endpoint']}/customindex/llm-calls-logs"
    cert_files = ("secrets/client.crt", "secrets/client.key")

    response = requests.put(
        url,
        data=json.dumps(payload),
        headers={"Content-Type": "application/json"},
        cert=cert_files,
        verify="secrets/server-ca.crt"
    )
    response.raise_for_status()
    print("Log entry sent successfully.")

  4. Verify in SAP Cloud Logging Service

    Once your application is sending logs via PUT, access the dashboard endpoint (provided in the service key file) and follow these steps to verify the data in SAP Cloud Logging Service:

    1. Open the Discover tab in SAP Cloud Logging Service.
    2. Select the logs-json-* index to locate your ingested data.
    3. Alternatively, create queries or dashboards to track usage, latency, and error rates.

    For more details, refer to the JSON ingestion documentation.

5. Best Practices

Regardless of your chosen ingestion approach, keep in mind:

  1. Initialize Only Once
    If using OTel, avoid multiple calls to your initialization function. Repeated setups can cause duplicate logging or performance overhead.

  2. Secure Your Credentials
    Whether using OTel or JSON ingestion, treat your service key, certificates, and private keys as sensitive.

6. Conclusion

Observability is the backbone of robust, efficient GenAI LLM solutions—helping you diagnose issues, and maintain strong service performance. With SAP Cloud Logging Service, you have a powerful, scalable environment to store and analyze logs, metrics, and traces from any environment or runtime. Meanwhile, both the OpenTelemetry approach and the JSON Endpoint approach can reliably feed your GenAI telemetry data into the service.

By choosing the ingestion method that fits your needs, and implementing best practices around security and dashboards, you’ll ensure your LLM-powered applications deliver timely, accurate, and compliant responses at scale.

Further Reading

  • SAP Help (SAP Cloud Logging)
    https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-logging/cloud-logging/what-is-sap-cloud-logging
  • OpenTelemetry Documentation
    https://opentelemetry.io/

entry.title: Re: S_PROGNAM Authorization check always successful
entry.content:
Hi @AlpErTunga 

should user be able to use SA38 at all? If not you can just omit SA38 in the auth objecct S_TCODE to prevent user from starting this transaction in the first place.

If user should be able to use SA38 but not be able to run certain reports (basically configure S_PROGNAM to a list of reports the user are not allowed to run) you are out of luck since there is no way of excluding specific values, only include them. Comparing the F4 help on select options where you can include material number 4711, 4713 and exclude 4712. In PFCG you can only allow materialnumber 4711 and 4713 but not exclude 4712.

If the reports you want to exclude are "within a range" like having the same starting names you can add a range of valid report names and by this "exclude" said range you want the user to not be able to start like mentioned in the postings here https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-discussions/how-to-exclude-values-from-field-begru/td-p/9334130 and here https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/exclude-values-in-authorosation-object-in-rsecadmin/qaq-p/4807201

However I feel like you should not do this because it's not only a big risk of missing some reports but also a ton of work to maintain. 

Seperatly the "Role maintenance is about Inclusions and not Exclusions". I suggest to make a role specifically designed for running SA38 with all the reports user should be able to run (if they need to use SA38) and include all the reports they should be able to run in the S_PROGNAME object and then assign this role to those few user.

Regards radinator

entry.title: Re: BDC for ME01
entry.content:
• EORD-LIFNR(01)
  • EORD-LIFNR(02)
  • EORD-LIFNR(03)
  • etc.

That's exactly how you fill subsequent lines with BDC. What is not working?

entry.title: SAP Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Join us for an insightful webinar on "SAP Business Data Cloud," where we explore the transformative potential of this innovative solution for data-driven enterprises.

The session will delve into the significance and functionalities of SAP Business Data Cloud, highlighting its pivotal role in enhancing data management and analytics.

Register Here: https://events.sap.com/sap-btp-2025/en/home or Live on YouTube

entry.title: Re: SAP Certification Transformation: How You Can Get and Stay Certified
entry.content:
Hello @Suzanne_Heiob and community,

it's me again! I have another question for you.

I bought my SAP Learning Hub subscription and I see that I have 4 included certification exam attempts per year to get and stay certified.

But I also know that to stay certified I can do an assessment that has unlimited attempts.

So I don't understand...are 4 attempts only to get a new certification? and unlimited attempst to stay certified?

Please, let me know. Thank you.

Valeria

entry.title: Re: BAPI_OBJCL_GETDETAIL
entry.content:
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zrr_test.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*

DATA: lt_bapi1003    TYPE TABLE OF bapi1003_alloc_values_num,
      lt_values_char TYPE TABLE OF bapi1003_alloc_values_char,
      ls_values_char TYPE   bapi1003_alloc_values_char,
      lt_values_curr TYPE TABLE OF bapi1003_alloc_values_curr,
      lt_bapiret2    TYPE TABLE OF bapiret2,
      ls_bapiret2    TYPE bapiret2.
DATA: ls_bapi1003  TYPE bapi1003_alloc_values_num,
      ls_objkey    TYPE bapi1003_key-object,
      ls_otable    TYPE bapi1003_key-objecttable VALUE 'MCH1',
      ls_classnum  TYPE bapi1003_key-classnum    VALUE 'GLOBAL_BATCH_CLASS',
      ls_classtype TYPE bapi1003_key-classtype   VALUE '023'.

BREAK rgarnepudi.

DATA:lv_objectkey_long  TYPE  bapi1003_key-object_long.
DATA:lv_len TYPE i.
DATA(lv_matnr) = 'ENDITEMDELIVERABLE-AURORA'.
**lv_matnr = lv_matnr+0(18).

lv_objectkey_long(40) = lv_matnr.
lv_objectkey_long+40(4) = '2550'.
lv_objectkey_long+40(10) = '0000001944'.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_OBJCL_GETDETAIL'
  EXPORTING
    objectkey        = ls_objkey
    objecttable      = ls_otable
    classnum         = ls_classnum
    classtype        = ls_classtype
    keydate          = sy-datum
    unvaluated_chars = ' '
    language         = sy-langu
    objectkey_long   = lv_objectkey_long
**  IMPORTING
**    status           =
**    standardclass    =
  TABLES
    allocvaluesnum   = lt_bapi1003
    allocvalueschar  = lt_values_char
    allocvaluescurr  = lt_values_curr
    return           = lt_bapiret2.

ls_values_char-charact = 'BATCH_NUMBER'.       "Characteristics
ls_values_char-value_char_long = '123456789'.        "characteristics value
APPEND ls_values_char TO lt_values_char.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_OBJCL_CHANGE'
  EXPORTING
    objectkey_long     = lv_objectkey_long
    objecttable        = ls_otable
    classnum           = ls_classnum
    classtype          = ls_classtype
  TABLES
    allocvaluesnumnew  = lt_bapi1003
    allocvaluescharnew = lt_values_char
    allocvaluescurrnew = lt_values_curr
    return             = lt_bapiret2.

READ TABLE lt_bapiret2 INTO ls_bapiret2 WITH KEY type = 'S'.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
  CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT'
    EXPORTING
      wait = 'X'.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: BDC for ME01
entry.content:
Hello @Mahil 

Alternatively to BDC for ME01, you can use the ME05 transaction or the SRCLST01 IDoc to mass create source lists.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Production Over Confirmation
entry.content:
Hi,

I've been tasked with finding a way to block operators from confirming a yield higher than the expected target quantity. How would this be possible?

Also, is it a simple 'yes' or 'no' when it comes to allowing over confirmations or is it possible to set tolerances (like 10% over the expected yield is acceptable).

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Subitem Generation is not happening in Location/Produ...
entry.content:
Hi @metschies ,

could you please explain what you have done to perform the SLOC replacement? We have the same problem and even after checking the mentioned sap note, we are still facing the issue, that abc does not perform the SLOC replacement as configured.

entry.title: Lift and Shift Migration to SAP RISE private edition, version consideration
entry.content:
Hello, we have a customer that is currently on the process of migrating the SAP infrastructure to SAP Rise.

The source S/4 HANA version is 2020.

In SAP Material we found the following limitation considering versions of S/4 HANA:
Releases with at least 9 months of maintenance (mainstream or extended) remaining are supported. For lower releases, an upgrade must be completed prior to migration to PCE. Customers are required to upgrade to the latest version of S/4HANA well before the end of mainstream or extended maintenance.

Considering that mainstream support for S/4 HANA ends on 31st December 2025 is there a final date, by which we should have completed the lift and shift migration of the customer's system?

entry.title: Re: No data in SAP BUILD Preview
entry.content: Hi Dan, I am having same issue. i already follow all the step that you gave. but when i preview the apps, the data is not showing.

entry.title: Needed ! SAC Story Table "unhide" a hidden row
entry.content:
Hi,

Problem is:
-using SAC with Live Model and a table.
-the table offers the feature "Hide row" to hide away rows defined by the developer ( not filtering !!!).

How to unhide hidden values ? 
How or where to get (accidently) hidden values back without setting up the table from new ?

ThanXs 
Martin

entry.title: How to pass dynamic value through a mdk Extension code
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have a requirement in SSAM 2405 where we need to pass a dynamic image as a Base64 string between MDK and an Extension (both ways).

Scenario:
I trigger an MDK extension from MDK, which opens the extension page. At some point inside the extension, I need to use an MDK action to trigger a customized Document Editor to edit an image using this Base64 String. The image needs to be passed from the extension to MDK for editing. After editing, the modified image must be passed back from MDK to the extension.

Can you please suggest and help me to achieve this?

Regards,

Divan Wellalage

entry.title: Re: Changeset processing in CAP Node.JS
entry.content:
I'm trying to set up something similar, where I want to select multiple items and use an action to process them together.

Even when using the ChangeSet option, the action logic is executed separately for each item, however, I've noticed that the HTTP header 'x-correlation-id' is the same for all selected items.

Before going ahead, I'd like someone else to confirm: can I use this header to identify all items that were selected?

See the annotation and the custom logic below:

{
    $Type : 'UI.DataFieldForAction',
    Label : 'Batch Action',
    Action: 'ApiService.batchAction',
    InvocationGrouping : #ChangeSet
}
this.on('batchAction', async (req) => {
      console.log('batchAction begin');
      console.log(req.headers['x-correlation-id']);
      console.log(req.params);
      console.log('batchAction end');
});

Regards,

Pedro

entry.title: Re: Global value in sap mdg fpm : mv_fpm_edit_mode
entry.content: Hi Jens, Thanks for the suggestion and if i want to segregate in process_event, Can i know is there any global variable for EDIT so that i can differentiate ?

entry.title: Re: can i edit my own blog?
entry.content:
@oliver_nuernberg Please make sure you join the Life Sciences group first: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-for-life-sciences/gh-p/life-sciences

Joining the group should give you the rights to edit it again. If you still cannot edit it after joining the group, please send us an email at community@sap.com so that we can help troubleshoot.

entry.title: SAP BusinessOne Webclient Extension in PHP (non-BTP)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

We are working on a web client extension by modifying an existing loosely coupled app we developed, which has a PHP backend and a UI5 frontend. However, we're having some challenges with authentication: we want to seamlessly authenticate users with their logged-in SAP credentials, avoiding the need for them to log in twice (once to the web client and again to our app).

Could anyone point us to relevant examples or documentation on how to achieve this?

Thanks,
Adrien

entry.title: S4H-Conversion Quell-Ledger
entry.content:
Hallo zusammen,

ich bräuchte dringend eine Einschätzung zu folgendem Thema:

Im Schritt R21 der Migration kam der Fehler FINS_RECON 108 (00 1000 2012 GLT0_BCK: Auf Ledger 00 gebuchte Salden werden nicht migriert) für bestimmte GJ hoch. Daraufhin habe ich die Einstellungen wie folgt angepasst:

Ledger 0L Buchungskreis * GJ 1900 Quellledger 0L

Ledger 0L Buchungskreis 1000 GJ 2012 Quellledger 00 (diesen Eintrag habe ich hinzugefügt)

Der Fehler war dann weg und es kamen auch keine Fehler bzgl. der Salden hoch, ebenso die Nachweislisten haben immer übereingestimmt.

Das Problem ist, dass der Kunde nie die alte Hauptbuchhaltung im Einsatz hatte und in der GLT0 gar keine IST-Werte gespeichert sind. Laut (womöglich falschem) Customizing sollte er ja aber alles ab 2012 aus der GLT0 ziehen und nicht aus der FAGLFLEXT. Er muss aber irgendwie den hinzugefügten Customizing-Eintrag ignoriert und nur den Eintrag mit Quelledger 0L beachtet haben, da definitiv Werte da sind und die Nachweislisten passen.

Hat hierfür jemand eine Erklärung? Sollte das Customizing kurz vor der Produktiven Conversion wieder zurückgedreht werden, sodass nur der erste Eintrag vorhanden ist, da ja die Nachweislisten passen oder würdet ihr das Customizing beibehalten?

Ich bin da momentan etwas ratlos.

Danke vorab für eine Einschätzung!

entry.title: Re: Badges are not showing
entry.content:
@Basant_Joshi - from what I can tell, you have not completed the community tutorial. Please retake it and make sure all the steps have been marked done/complete. 
If the error still persists after completing the tutorial again, please send an email to community@sap.com so that we can help troubleshoot.

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to detect MRP failure? Also, is there...
entry.content:
Hello @SI_y_Ito ,

Thanks for your question.

In an SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation, monitoring and managing Material Requirements Planning (MRP) runs is crucial for ensuring smooth operations. Here’s how you can recognize MRP failures and intentionally simulate a failure for testing purposes:

1.Schedule MRP Runs App:
- As you mentioned, the "Schedule MRP Runs" app provides a status for each MRP run. A "failed" status indicates that the MRP run did not complete successfully. You can monitor this app regularly to check for any failures.

2.Error Logs:

- Check the error logs for detailed information about why an MRP run failed. This can be accessed through the application logs in the SAP Fiori Launchpad. Look for specific error messages that can guide you in troubleshooting the issue.

3.Notifications:
- Set up notifications or alerts for MRP failures. This can be done through the SAP Fiori Launchpad by configuring alerts for specific events or statuses.

To intentionally cause an MRP failure for testing purposes, you can manipulate certain conditions that are known to cause errors:

1. Master Data Issues:
- Introduce inconsistencies or errors in master data, such as missing or incorrect material master records, BOMs, or work center data. This can lead to MRP failures due to data validation errors.

2.Capacity Constraints:
- Set unrealistic capacity constraints or scheduling parameters that cannot be met. This can cause the MRP run to fail due to infeasibility.

3.System Configuration:
- Alter system configuration settings related to MRP, such as planning horizons or lot-sizing procedures, to values that are known to cause issues.

4. Authorization Errors:
- Remove necessary authorizations for the user executing the MRP run. This can lead to authorization errors and cause the run to fail.

When intentionally causing failures, ensure that you are working in a test or sandbox environment to avoid disrupting actual business operations. Additionally, document the changes made so they can be easily reverted after testing.

Best Regards

Dave

entry.title: Account Assignment in TRM Financial Transactions - WBS Element
entry.content:
This article describes how to use WBS Element in financial transactions in Treasury and Risk Management (TRM) of in S/4HANA Public Cloud, mainly focus on using WBS element in account assignments which will influence the posting journals arising from TRM transactions.

1. When creating transactions for certain product categories (600, 760, 620), a WBS element can be entered in sub-area Additional Fields of Administr. tab.

For example, below is a FX forward transaction (product category 600):

When doing so in the transactions of product category 600 or 760 (OTC FX option), the major purpose is using WBS element as a differentiation criterion in hedge management to match the FX exposure and FX derivative at the level of WBS element. For more details regarding differentiation criteria in hedge management, please refer to help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/618b3d58eaa89144e10000000a4450e5.html?locale=en-US.

Unlike cost center, the column of WBS element is unavailable in position indicator by default, unless that WBS element is enabled as a differentiation term through configuration activity 104115 Define and Assign Differentiations (which will be introduced later). That means, WBS element can't even be manually entered in the Position Indicator of TRM transactions, and consequently, the WBS element (entered in sub-area Additional Fields of Administr. tab) will NOT be carried over to the posting journals generated from the transaction. In a word, it has no impact on postings.

2. Through the app Additional Account Assignments - Substitution Rules, you can create a substitution rule for WBS Element from TRM side.

This app provides comprehensive preconditions (characteristics from TRM transactions) which can help to determine the WBS element based on complicated combinations of multiple characteristics from TRM transactions.

Just one simple example:

The substitution rule defined in this app take effect when TRM is transferring data to accounting interface, therefore, the determined WBS element is not reflected in any part of the TRM transactions.

Usually, the determined WBS element via this app will apply to journal items which are posted with primary cost element that requires cost object like WBS element, cost center. If both of cost center and WBS element are determined for the same journal item, then the actual posting will be recorded in WBS element, and cost center will be the role of statistical posting.

For more details of this app, please refer to help document https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/e5ec5859d8e54df98492d80564a734c0/b06cfc3ba30a46809a181952bb6b78f4.html?locale=en-US.

3. Through configuration activity 104115 (Define and Assign Differentiations), you can define a differentiation which includes term WBS Element, and then assign this differentiation to proper valuation area(s) and/or accounting code(s).

Afterwards, when creating a financial transaction within the related accounting code or for related valuation area, in the tab Administr., you will see the field WBS Element within the subarea Position Assignment. And this WBS Element field is mandatory to be entered with a value.

The WBS Element entered in sub-area Position Assignment of Administr. tab is stored in the position indicator of this transaction. In the position indicator, the WBS Element column is grayed out, because it must be identical with the WBS Element specified in sub-area Position Assignment of Administr. tab.

Consequently, the WBS Element stored in position indicator will be carried over to posting journal items generated from this transaction, and the WBS element will apply to the journal items which are posted with primary cost element that requires cost object like WBS element, cost center. If both of cost center and WBS element are determined for the same journal item, then the actual posting will be recorded in WBS element, and cost center will be the role of statistical posting.

As mentioned, the WBS element field is available by default in the subarea Additional Field in tab Administr. of the transactions of certain product categories 600, 760, 620. For the transactions of a product category other than 600, 760, 620, you can enable WBS element as a differentiation via Define and Assign Differentiations, then WBS element field will be available in the subarea Position Assignment in tab Administr.. This method is useful when requiring separate and manual determination of the WBS element and store it in tab Administr. of the transaction, and this WBS element should be used to record the profit/loss arising from this transaction from accounting perspective.

The important thing is that the decision to use Define and Assign Differentiations should be made cautiously, as it not only makes the WBS element field visible in transactions but also has several significant impacts on transaction processing and accounting, which need to be thoroughly evaluated before configuration:

  • For every transaction within the valuation area and/or accounting code which is assigned with this differentiation, WBS element field will be a mandatory field to entered in financial transactions.
  • Position and quantity of financial transactions will be split according to WBS elements in addition to predefined differentiations, this effect is especially obvious for financial transactions and class positions of securities (e.g. bond, stock).
  • Because of above point, the profit/loss (for example, realized gain/loss when derecognition) of securities class positions will be measured and accounted in line with each WBS element.

entry.title: SAP PaPM Cloud with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello!

I have a question regarding the usage of PaPM Cloud with S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. In this setup, does PaPM Cloud use the HANA database of S/4HANA or is it based on a separate SAP HANA Cloud database within SAP BTP?

Best regards,
Kaiwen

SAP Profitability and Performance Management

entry.title: Eclipse ADT - 'Apply Pseudo Comment' is not working for ATC finding
entry.content:
Hi Community,

actually we are perfoming the custom code migration for S/4HANA. We are using the ABAP Development Tools (ADT) in Eclipse to execute ATC checks and applying quickfixes. There is also a functionallity to apply a pseudo comment for an ATC finding, if available. But this function doesn't work. Nothing happens on execution.

Eclipse and ADT are on the latest Version. Does anybody faced the same issue and has an idea to fix this?

Thanks very much for responses.

Best regards,
Christoph

entry.title: Re: SAP Build App subscription failed
entry.content: I have the same issue. Tried just now running booster as well as trying to manually subscribe the SAP Build Apps service.

entry.title: Re: How SAP Public Cloud Customize MRP Logic?
entry.content:
Hello @Frank036,

Thanks for your question.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, especially the public cloud version, the ability to customize or modify standard functionalities like MRP logic is more restricted compared to the on-premise version. This is due to the cloud's multi-tenant nature, which requires maintaining a standardized core to ensure stability and ease of updates across all customers.

However, there are still some other workarounds you can consider to address your client's requirement:

1. Use of Standard Features:
- Demand Management:You can manage unplanned orders by using different demand management strategies. For example, you can classify R&D orders differently and manage them outside the standard MRP process.
- Order Types: If possible, use different order types for R&D orders and configure the system to exclude these from MRP runs. This might involve setting specific parameters or using user exits if available.

2. Business Add-Ins (BAdIs):
- In the public cloud, the extensibility options are limited, but SAP provides certain BAdIs that can be used to influence MRP logic. Check if there are any available BAdIs that can be used to filter out specific order types from MRP calculations.

3.Custom Logic in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:
- Use the "Custom Logic" app to implement custom business logic. This app allows you to add custom code to certain business processes. However, this is subject to the available enhancement options provided by SAP.

 - If none of the above solutions are feasible, consider reaching out to SAP Support or your SAP Customer Success Manager. They might provide insights into upcoming features or alternative solutions.

It's important to carefully evaluate the impact of any changes or workarounds on your client's business processes and ensure that they align with the overall system architecture and business goals.

Hope it can help.

Best Regards

Dave

entry.title: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2501 (including 2412)
entry.content:
Welcome to this highlights blog of our last two releases including:
  • Quickstart FMEA assessments – Reuse strategy assessments for classes to speed up FMEA creation.
  • Specify or find the origin of recommendations.
  • Long texts in alerts and notifications to enhance context and clarity.
  • Rule chaining – Execute rules sequentially to improve accuracy and avoid timing issues.
  • Direct navigation from alerts to technical objects to enable quick access to equipment details.
  • Public APIs for Alerts and Alert Types – Manage alerts and categories via the SAP Business Acceleration Hub.

Check out the videos below to see the features in action and don't forget to register here to join our 2502 webinar. If you register for an IAM webcast, you'll be automatically subscribed to our IAM Community Engagement Newsletter.

Let's dive in.

 

Asset Reliability Engineering

Quickstart FMEA Assessments Using Strategy Assessments for Classes

Creating an FMEA (Failure Modes and Effects Analysis) assessment from scratch can be time-consuming, but there's a smarter way to reuse existing strategy assessments to quickly build your failure data hierarchy.

You can quick start an FMEA assessment by copying failure data and recommendations from a Strategy Assessment for Class, filtered based on the class and failure data profile of the technical object.

  • Technical objects must share the same Class, Planning Plant, and Failure Data Profile

  • At least one released Strategy Assessment for Class exists for the selected class.

  • There are no duplicate failure modes between the source and FMEA assessment (to prevent copy failure).

Watch the video for more information and see the SAP Help Portal Quick Start of FMEA Assessments Using Strategy Assessments for Classes.

Specifying and Tracking Recommendation Origins

Being able to track or maintain recommendation origins enhances transparency and decision-making. Whether recommendations stem from legal/regulatory requirements, hazard operations, preventive maintenance reviews, quality standards or another source, specifying origins ensures better traceability and you can maintain multiple origins for each recommendation.

Origins are maintained in SAP S/4HANA. Once the recommendation is created, the object page opens in edit mode, where you can modify origins as long as the recommendation status is "In Process", "Created", or "Implementation in Process".

You can comfortably find recommendations from a specific source, such as quality standards using the filter and also add the Origin column to the list view where you can see all origins of all recommendations in one view.

Check out the video to see it in action.

 

Asset Health Monitoring

Enhancing Maintenance Decisions with Long Texts in Alerts

You can create and utilize "long texts" for alert types to provide clear, actionable context, enabling users to make faster, more informed decisions, thus improving operational efficiency and reducing resolution times. The feature includes:

  • Long Text Support: Alerts now include a long text field (up to 5000 characters) with rich text formatting, including clickable links, bullet points, and indentation.
  • Contextual Information: Provide essential details to guide troubleshooting and decision-making, such as actions, measures, or failure patterns.
  • Embedded URLs: Add links to additional resources or documentation.
  • API Enhancements:
    • Alert Type API: Read, create, and update long text for Alert Types.
    • Alert API: Read, create, and update long text for individual Alert instances.
  • Notification Integration: Pre-fill long text from Alerts into Notifications, with character count validation.

 Watch the video to find out more.

 

Rule Chaining for Sequential Execution*

Improve accuracy and reliability of rule execution by triggering rules based on the completion of other rules. This new feature eliminates timing issues and simplifies managing complex rule dependencies. We have introduced the following:

  • Chained Rules: Rules can now execute sequentially with one rule triggering the next.
  • Derivation & Aggregation: Chain both derivation and aggregation rules seamlessly.
  • Monitoring Alerts: Add a monitoring rule at the end of the chain to raise alerts.

In addition to schedule-based execution, triggering rules through a chain ensures reliable indicator values and avoids race conditions, making rule management efficient and dependable.

With this enhancement, you gain better control and consistency in sequential processes. Watch the video:

 

*only applicable for eIoT

 

New Navigation: Faster Access from Alerts to Technical Objects

We've introduced a new navigation feature that allows you to seamlessly access equipment details directly from alerts. The Technical Object in both the Alert List and Alert Details Page is now clickable, enabling direct navigation to the Explore Technical Objects app.

Benefits:

  • Faster Access to relevant equipment information.
  • Improved Efficiency by eliminating manual searches.
  • Streamlined Navigation for quicker handling and analysis of alerts.

This update simplifies your workflow, making it easier than ever to analyze alerts and access the details you need.

 

Public APIs for Alert Type and Alert

The Manage Alerts and Manage Alerts Types API are available now for public consumption on the SAP Business Acceleration Hub.

  • Manage Alerts: The API enables you to manage the alerts in SAP Asset Performance Management. Alerts help you with any potential failure that may occur or has already occurred for the technical object and helps to identify potential actions to be taken for the incoming alerts
  • Manage Alert Types: This API represents categories of master data objects to plan maintenance activities for technical objects. Create alert types with a unique ID, descriptions, and define Origin and Severity to support informed decisions and effective action planning.

For more information, see Alert and Alert Types Packages

 

Keep In Touch

Here's a quick reminder how to keep in touch with all things APM:

  • Check out all past and present Intelligent Asset Management webcasts here. If you register for an IAM webcast, you will be automatically subscribed to our IAM Community Engagement Newsletter. 
  • Check some of our recent blogs:
    • Why Failure-Mode Focus Matters in Intelligent Asset Management 
    • SAP Asset Performance Management Embedding Cumulocity IoT to Drive Innovations in IoT and AI
    • How AI-based Anomaly Detection empowers reliability engineers in SAP Asset Performance Management
  • Subscribe to What's New on the SAP Help Portal
  • Submit or vote for the improvement you would like to see in the SAP Continuous Influence session

See you again soon for the 2502 release update!

we want all users created in transaction SU01 to have a security policy entered.
Therefore, this should be made a mandatory field.
Unfortunately I couldn't find anything about it on the Internet.

Does anyone know how to do this?

Many thanks in advance
Thomas

entry.title: Re: Needed ! SAC Story Table "unhide" a hidden row
entry.content:
Got the answer:

Unfortunatley i found it only in german in SAP Help:
- Hilfe zu SAP Analytics Cloud  - Q1 2025 (2025.1 )  -Ändern von Tabellen: 
- Set in the Table in the Table Sandwich (...) Menue the Table Details:

-Go to th detail ans hit the <XX> Hide Out Number Text

entry.title: how to change Length of G/L Account Number in custom CDS view
entry.content:
Customer has adopted 8-digit Length of G/L account and in custom CDS view level, the length of G/L account is 10 Digits. I_GLAccountInCompanyCode shows the length is 10. how to change the length of G/L account so it can flow in the integration to show the length as 8 digits.

Thank You.

Regards,

Annapurna

entry.title: Foreign Currency Valuation (FAGL_FCV) without using document splitting
entry.content:
When we run FAGL_FCV the P&L postings to the unrealized gains/losses accounts are picking the CO per the original documents. This is causing some problems because we'd like to close some of the Cost Centres, or it's trying to post to Internal Orders which don't have any budget for the current year.

We just want this these to post using a default Cost Centre as defined in OKB9.

Is this possible?

entry.title: Re: Does SAC have a Rank function?
entry.content: Hi,

entry.title: Re: Calibration Sessions from Prior Years
entry.content:
@Jennifer10 

If you dont want the previous calibration session it can be deleted but please note below points.

Note: Deleting a Calibration session will permanently delete the session. There is no way to restore a deleted session

  • Changes made to Live Profile data during the Calibration session will be deleted.
  • Changes to Performance Management or Compensation forms will not be affected
  • If a Calibration session is created using Live Profile data from a previous Calibration session, deleting the previous session will not change the ratings or rating source information for an active session. 
  • Please test above case in your test environment before concluding as the final step.

Please mark this as answer if this help

Thanks

Mridul

entry.title: Re: Does SAC have a Rank function?
entry.content: Hi Madhu, Model level this measure doesn't contain any data you have to put data in story level as per your requirement. I will add steps.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to read the data from analytical view...
entry.content:
In Q1 2024 SAP released "Data Actions – cross-model copy with analytic models (SAC owned) as a source" which I hope they will expand further on.

Maybe @Max_Gander can comment better on roadmap & timing, but I heard one of the next goals of Datasphere/SAC  planning integration could very well be to allow for a data actions capability (LINK) to be able to run (read) data from (live?) Analytic models or directly from DataSphere Analytical views, and store results back into your planning models.

This would be very valuable to copy-in latest actuals without import job (although you still need it for master data sync), but even better: to perform advanced calculations for your planning models (for example: to apply inflation rates) completely eliminating the need to bring in this reference data into a model in SAC.

entry.title: Attchment in the VL03N transaction.
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have an issue where I can not create an attachment in the VL03N transaction. The "Attachment List' is greyed out. Any ide how to enable it?

entry.title: Re: Global value in sap mdg fpm : mv_fpm_edit_mode
entry.content:
Hi Karthik,

at time point PROCESS_EVENT, the global edit mode is not yet updated in the central FPM runtime instance. So, calling method IF_FPM~GET_UIBB_EDIT_MODE will not necessarily give you reliable result.

Best regards,
Jens

entry.title: Re: Global value in sap mdg fpm : mv_fpm_edit_mode
entry.content: Hi Karthik, at time point PROCESS_EVENT, the global edit mode is not yet updated in the central FPM runtime instance. So, calling method IF_FPM~GET_UIBB_EDIT_MODE will not necessarily give you reliable result. Best regards, Jens

entry.title: Re: Duplicate plan order orders for same sales order line...
entry.content:
Hi Houda,

FYI & please refer below available details

The user has converted the plan orders to production order . Therefore unable to share the details in MD04.

However please see the below screen shot of COOIS report and assignment tab of production orders . You can see the duplicated production orders for same Sales Order Line Item (20). If you need more details please let me know.

 

See the different planned orders for same line item in the assignment tab.

1st Production Order

 

2nd Production Order

entry.title: Re: UI5 Fragment Changes Not Reflecting Even After Cache...
entry.content: Thank you! When I added     data-sap-ui-xx-viewCache="false"    in index.html , the problem was solved

entry.title: Re: Analytic Model was created with older version of the editor
entry.content: We opened a ticket on that issue and have now a solution. We changed the technical names of assoziations in the facts and also the assoziation for one dimension before the disruption. After naming the technical names of the assoziations back to the original naming and changing back the assoziation for one dimension, the analytical models can now be accessed again without an error message. Maybe also a solution for you and way faster, than a complete rebuilt of the AMs

entry.title: Re: ABAP are you listening? Apache ActiveMQ is calling
entry.content:
ECC SAP system events have been able to be kicked off from outside of SAP for quite a long time w/o external software, e.g., see below for a real working example that I have used personally.  https ports can be used, too.

“http://<hostname>:8000/sap/bc/batch/event_raise?sap-client=800&eventid=ZZZ_SM62_DEFINED_TRIGGER&eventparm=<parameter>&targetinstance=B”

entry.title: Re: What is end user training, and why is it so important?
entry.content: Do you have any manuals or guides for end users?

entry.title: Re: Duplicate plan order orders for same sales order line...
entry.content:
Hi Catetano,

The first production order has created on 30th Jan and then second production order has created on 31st Jan. As per the config (OMDW) the rescheduling horizon has defined as 100 days.

entry.title: Re: Tutorial: Creating Configurable Materials (KMAT) in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: @mickaelquesnotWell explained but but adding screenshots to illustrate the steps would help blog readers understand better and make your blog more accessible to everyone.

entry.title: Re: [ChaRM] Need to change change cycle assignement in mass
entry.content:
Hello Slimdre, 

There was never a report to change from an active cycle to another active cycle. Report /TMWFLOW/CRM_CHNG_DOC_REASSIGN was used to assign changes in closed cycle to a followup active cycle from the same project.

But in 7.2 the concept has been completely redesigned hence the report /TMWFLOW/CRM_CHNG_DOC_REASSIGN is not longer needed.

Customer must assign changes manually in CRM UI. There is no other way to do that.

You can refer to below document.

https://help.sap.com/viewer/8b923a2175be4939816f0981b73856c7/7.2.10/en-US/4c4da0c3b5c76f82e10000000a42189e.html

Best regards,

Dolores

entry.title: Fiori Elements ListReport with OData V4 - Cannot hide the Column with UI.lineItem.hidden
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to build ListReport (Fiori Elements) using OData V4 based on CDS Views and UI Annotations.

For ListReport, I have used @ui.listItem[{position:10}] to display a column on the List.

To hide the column, I can use @ui.listItem[{position: 10, hidden: true}]. This works fine.

But I would like to set the visibility of Column based on a Variable like:

@UI.listItem[{position: 10, hidden: #(VARIABLE_NAME)}

When VARIABLE_NAME is true, the column should be hidden. But this does not work as expected.

Currently, the column is still displayed in the Table, but Value of the column is blank.

Expected Result: Whole of the Column should be Hidden.

If I pass, proper boolean value of true or false to hidden, it works as expected.

Why I am using @ui.lineItem.hidden ? - This is because, it is required to sometimes, hide the column in the list, but display on the objectPage (next page).

UI5 Versions tries: 1.108, 1.120, 1.122

Any inputs or insights can help me a lot.

Thank you.

Regards,

Avinash Ambalgi

entry.title: Re: [SAP BTP Onboarding Series] Joule – Getting Started with Joule and SAP SuccessFactors

We checked with the Customer that "SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition" is already assigned.

Best regards

Tomasz

entry.title: Re: Excel Data to Internal table using ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE(obsolete) without additional cod
entry.content:
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zrr_test.
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
TYPES:BEGIN OF  ty_format,
        f01 TYPE char30,  " PO Number
        f02 TYPE char30,  " PO Item
        f03 TYPE char30,  " Document Date (BLDAT)
        f04 TYPE char30,  " Posting Date (BUDAT)
        f05 TYPE char30,  " Delivery Note (XBLNR)
        f06 TYPE char30,  " Material (MATNR)
        f07 TYPE char30,  " Movement Type (BWART)
        f08 TYPE char30,  " Special Stock Indicator (SOBKZ
        f09 TYPE char30,  " SPlant (WERKS)
        f10 TYPE char30,  " Storage Location (LGORT)
        f11 TYPE char30,  " Quantity (MENGE)
        f12 TYPE char50,  " Storage Bin (EWM_LGPLA)
        f13 TYPE char50,  " Batch Number (If relevant) (CHARG_SID)
        f14 TYPE char50,  " External Batch number (if relevant) (ATWRT)
        f15 TYPE char50,  " Shelf Life Expiration Date (VFDAT)-YYYYMMDD
        f16 TYPE char50,  " Serial Number (If relevant) (SERNR)
        f17 TYPE char30,  " IUID (If relevant) (UII)
      END OF  ty_format.
DATA:gt_format TYPE TABLE OF ty_format.  " Download file Using this Format
DATA:gs_format TYPE ty_format.  " Download file Using this Format
DATA:lv_file TYPE rlgrap-filename,
     lt_file TYPE TABLE OF  alsmex_tabline.
lv_file = 'File Path'.
CALL FUNCTION 'ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE'
  EXPORTING
    filename                = lv_file
    i_begin_col             = '1'
    i_begin_row             = 2
    i_end_col               = 17
    i_end_row               = 65536
  TABLES
    intern                  = lt_file
  EXCEPTIONS
    inconsistent_parameters = 1
    upload_ole              = 2
    OTHERS                  = 3.
IF sy-subrc = 0.
  LOOP AT lt_file ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<lfs_file>).

    CASE <lfs_file>-col.
      WHEN '0001'.
        gs_format-f01 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0002'.
        gs_format-f02 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0003'.
        gs_format-f03 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0004'.
        gs_format-f04 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0005'.
        gs_format-f05 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0006'.
        gs_format-f06 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0007'.
        gs_format-f07 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0008'.
        gs_format-f08 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0009'.
        gs_format-f09 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0010'.
        gs_format-f10 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0011'.
        gs_format-f11 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0012'.
        gs_format-f12 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0013'.
        gs_format-f13 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0014'.
        gs_format-f14 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0015'.
        gs_format-f15 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0016'.
        gs_format-f16 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN '0017'.
        gs_format-f17 = <lfs_file>-value.
      WHEN OTHERS.
    ENDCASE.
    AT END OF row.
      APPEND gs_format TO gt_format.
      CLEAR:gs_format.
    ENDAT.

  ENDLOOP.
ENDIF.

entry.title: Re: Hi IS ODATA/Standard SOAP Available for SAP DMC SAP I...
entry.content:
SAP DM was both REST and OData APIs available (SOAP is not supported) and they are all documented here

Thanks

Kevin

entry.title: Re: Advance Shipment functionality in Service Order
entry.content:
Hi Sandeep,

very nice blog!

Stefan

entry.title: Fetch List of Fiori apps from a given CDS view
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I have a scenario to find out the list of Fiori apps that uses a specific custom CDS view exposed as ODATA service

Is there any way that I can find the relevant Fiori applications by a given custom CDS name?

Any suggestion will be helpful here. Thanks in advance

Regards,

RM

entry.title: how to identify which planning function uses certain variable?
entry.content:
Dear BW Experts,

i want to know the planning function technical name that uses a certain variable.

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/link-between-rszeltdir-and-rsrrepdir/qaq-p/12501099

In the link above, I can found how to identify the technical name of the query using a variable ID, but it is difficult to find the planning function in this way.

For example, I want to find out which copy function or distribution by reference function use a certain variable.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Re: S4 HANA 2023 Variant missing for ERP Readiness check in ATC System
entry.content: Hello, this note is only for 7.57; not for 7,58. do you have a idea?

entry.title: Re: 3 Reasons to Register for the SAP Business Data Cloud Primer in 30 Minutes Webinar
entry.content: I look forward to experiencing the evolution of analytics and its impact, gaining a broader perspective across different ecosystems and platforms.

entry.title: Re: Addon Connection Timeout in SAP B1
entry.content:
Hello,

The addon could not connect to SBO.

Try to insert some log in your code to know in each line the addon is stopping.

And please post here the lines of your code that connects to SBO.

Best regards

entry.title: Re: parameter 'args' is not of type array UI5 issue
entry.content: @DeeptiManjunath ok, keep your secrets, thats not how you will get any answer, are you actual an SAP employee?

entry.title: Re: One or more parameters could not be found. - sap.ui.core.theming.Parameters
entry.content: @DeeptiManjunath again, you do not provide any information so we can help you

entry.title: Re: Fetch List of Fiori apps from a given CDS view
entry.content:
You could try to lookup the CDS view in SE11 and then use the Where-Used search

Ok, nevermind. I tried this with the CDS view CSORDWLF1873 and only found my own report but not the Fiori App that was using it originally.

entry.title: Re: Fetch List of Fiori apps from a given CDS view
entry.content:
This is not easy. You basically need to find each service where this CDS View is used.

So use the where-used feature to check where the CDS is used and then need to find which Service is using this.
Maybe also use Code Search to find the string in code

entry.title: Re: SAP Stammtisch Chennai April 2023
entry.content:
Hi Moderator or Admin,
I am very interested in SAP & planning to switch my career into it. In the last few weeks, I have been doing my research to especially enter into the SAP domain & was looking forward for a similar community online to get my knowledge horizoned more with better professionals like you guys who has a vast amount of experience in the domain already. I would like to connect with you guys & gain valuable insights before choosing which module to choose and set realistic & right expectations so I can propel forward in my new journey in SAP domain. Also, would like to know if there are any other platforms like Whatsapp or Facebook groups where we have a similar group for knowledge sharing & also for helping with job assistance

entry.title: Re: Can we display an error message in the input field wh...
entry.content:
I don´t know in CAP Java, but in Nodejs is use this:

req.error(400, 'REQUIRED_FIELD', { field: 'newPrice' });

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Bin Capacity check based on Check Acc. to Key Figure Packaging Material
entry.content:
Hi Venu,

This config only support no. of HUs and not the weight. for weight capacity check you need to follow another config within Storage type.

 

Best Regards,
Anil

entry.title: Re: Mass programmatic deployment of Fiori Elements applications on S/4HANA 2021 On-Premise
entry.content: You want to deploy new Applications? I dont know of any way, but you need to do only once? How many applications are you talking about? Maybe create a custom generator

Hi Moderator or Admin,
I am very interested in SAP & planning to switch my career into it. I would like to connect with you guys & gain valuable insights before choosing which module to choose and set realistic & right expectations so I can propel forward in my new journey in SAP domain. Also, would like to know if there are any other platforms like Whatsapp or Facebook groups where we have a similar group for knowledge sharing & also for helping with job assistance.

entry.title: Extracting All Available Values for Restriction Fields Across All Restriction Types
entry.content:
Is there a way to extract the available options or selectable values for each restriction field in SAP?





------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Extracting All Available Values for Restriction Field...
entry.content: The IAM Information System application allows you to view details on the usage of business roles, business catalogs, business users, and restrictions, along with their interconnections.

entry.title: Hosting SAP CodeJam event in Poznan, Poland.
entry.content:
Dear SAP CodeJam team,

We would like to host SAP CodeJam at All For one in Poznan, Poland.

Our preferred topic is “Combining SAP Cloud Application Programming Model with SAP HANA Cloud to Build Full-Stack Applications.” We’re aiming for late April or May, but we’re flexible and can adjust to a different date if needed. The event will be designed for approximately 25 participants.

Br,
Julian

entry.title: Re: How do I make the Security Policy field a mandatory f...
entry.content:
Did you already try to code it within BADI_IDENTITY_CHECK ?

Could look like (written quickly, so adjust the code)

if not ( is_badi_identity_check-modus eq cl_identity_persistence=>co_ta_modus_create or
is_badi_identity_check-modus eq cl_identity_persistence=>co_ta_modus_modify ).
  return.
endif.

data: ls_logondata type suid_st_node_logondata,
      ls_msg       type symsg.

  try.

      call method is_badi_identity_check-idref->get_logondata
        importing
          es_logondata = ls_logondata.

      if ls_logondata-SECURITY_POLICY is not initial.
        return.
      else.
        clear: ls_msg.
        ls_msg-msgty = 'E'.
        ls_msg-msgid = 'ZXXXX'. " your class
        ls_msg-msgno = '999'. " your number
        call method io_badi_msg_buffer->add_object_message_symsg
          exporting
            iv_bname     = is_badi_identity_check-bname
            iv_nodename  = if_identity_definition=>gc_node_logondata
            iv_field     = if_identity_definition=>GC_FIELD_LOGONDATA_SEC_POLICY
            is_msg       = ls_msg
            iv_lifetime  = if_suid_msg_buffer=>co_lifetime_permanent.
      endif.
    catch cx_root.
  endtry.

entry.title: Re: Employee Central source of data
entry.content:
yes its internal to SF and all are now on HANA DB

source is of course SF as you update data its stored in its data model

its complete HR solution

entry.title: SAP BTP Mobile Services Native/MDK App Authorization Concept
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am currently working with SAP Mobile Services. I created Mobile Services instance on BTP. Established trust between these two platforms. Then I created native/MDK application that is utilizing Sample OData ESPM.

I kept all the default setting for the Security on the Mobile Cockpit for the application except for the enabling the anonymous access.

I created custom branded client and built it via Cloud Build. The app is installed to the android device. Nevertheless, when I install it, it is still asking for credentials to login to the Mobile Cockpit.

Is there or what is the way to distribute the application to people that will have access to neither BTP subaccount nor Mobile Cockpit.

I went through the documentation but is not really helpful for that topic.

Any feedback is really appreciated!

Br,
Aleks.

entry.title: How to make Vendor code and name always appear in the text field in FBL3N?
Re: Is it possible to read the data from analytical view...
entry.content:
Hi,

In the context of seamless planning we plan to let you reference fact tables and views from the SAP Datasphere space that your planning model is deployed to. With that you could:

  • Display data in the model data foundation, charts and tables
  • Copy data into plan versions / primary model tables (via version management or data action)
  • Reference virtual data in data actions and calculations
  • No write-back to the referenced tables

BR
Max

entry.title: Re: How to Send a Custom HTTP Header in All SAPUI5 OData V4 Calls?
entry.content: Check sap.ui.model.odata.v4.ODataModel#changeHttpHeaders

entry.title: Re: Mass maintenance of Risk ID and Rule ID for a Mitigat...
entry.content: I am referring to the Mitigation control Master Data to map the Risk vs Rules information in the Mitigation Controls

entry.title: Re: 'Free' text from classroom trainings registration
entry.content: The "label.Free" label ID could be tried. Additional details about the debug locale are available in the KBA 2913764.

entry.title: Difference in AP Report and Ledger Reconciliation
entry.content:
Dear Experts,
 
     We have found that, for same vendor, there is difference balance due
 
1. Aging report for same document showing (176321.040)
2. In Ledger statement showing Balance due 9372.00
 
Vendor is having single currency that is INR.
 
Please let us know the reason for the same.
 
 
 
Regards,
Gazanfar Ali Shaikh
